en.extract:
The gray-crested helmetshrike (Prionops poliolophus) is a species of bird in the Vanga family Vangidae, formerly usually included in the Malaconotidae.
It is found in Kenya and Tanzania. Its natural habitats are dry savanna and subtropical or tropical dry shrubland. It is threatened by habitat loss.
It has a clutch size of one egg.

The '''gray-crested helmetshrike''' ('''''Prionops poliolophus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the Vanga [[Family (taxonomy)|family]] [[Vangidae]], formerly usually included in the [[Malaconotidae]].

It is found in [[Kenya]] and [[Tanzania]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are dry [[savanna]] and subtropical or tropical dry [[shrubland]]. It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

It has a clutch size of one egg.<ref>{{Citation |chapter=Propagated Fish in Resource Management |date=2004 |editor-last=Fenner |editor-first=Daniel B. |publisher=American Fisheries Society |doi=10.47886/9781888569698.ch39 |editor2-last=Walsh |editor2-first=Maureen G. |editor3-last=Winkelman |editor3-first=Dana L. |isbn=978-1-888569-69-8 }}</ref>
